sound_type: powerful explosive blow -- V-shaped exhale, 3-4 m tall, audible from shore. Also low-frequency moans, pulses, knocking sounds. E=5
ecosystem: Near shore, coastal. Summer in Arctic; winter in Baja California. Migration follows PNW coast closely.
season: Southbound Oct-Jan. Northbound Mar-Jun. PCFG summer residents along PNW coast.

coordinates.description: Gray Whale along Washington coast. Near-shore migration. Westport/Long Beach observation areas. PCFG summer feeding.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST ESCHRICHTIIDAE -- monotypic family (one genus, one species). Third baleen whale family. Seventh marine mammal family. Ancient lineage. 13 consecutive marine mammals (180-192).
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Eschrichtiidae: Gray Whale (192) -- FIRST and only.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Eschrichtius from Daniel Frederik Eschricht, Danish zoologist. robustus from Latin 'robust, strong.' 'Gray Whale' for its mottled gray appearance.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Eschrichtius -- monotypic genus. Only one living species: E. robustus.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Cetacea. Third baleen whale family: Balaenopteridae (190-191), Eschrichtiidae (192).
taxonomy_note.family_position: Seventh marine mammal family: Phocidae, Otariidae, Mustelidae, Phocoenidae, Delphinidae, Balaenopteridae, Eschrichtiidae. 13 consecutive.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Marine mammal era: 13 consecutive (180-192). 7 families. E=1 to E=8. 5 feeding strategies.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 80-5,000+ Hz. Broadband blow plus low-frequency vocalizations.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Blow: broadband. Moans: 100-400 Hz. Pulses: 200-2,000 Hz. Knocking: 100-1,000 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Blow: 1-3 sec, 3-5 per surfacing. Moans: 1-5 sec. Pulses: brief trains.
song_metrics.note_structure: Blow (powerful exhalation), moans (low sustained), pulses (rhythmic), knocking (percussive).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Simple. No complex song structure. Functional vocalizations plus impressive blow.
song_metrics.timbre: Blow: explosive, deep whoosh. Moans: low, sustained. Pulses: rhythmic. Knocking: percussive.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Powerful blow audible from shore. Near-shore migration makes this the most audible cetacean from PNW land.
song_metrics.tempo: Blow pattern: 3-5 blows per surfacing, then dive. Vocalizations intermittent.
song_metrics.context: Coastal migration, near-shore feeding, calving lagoons, mother-calf communication.
song_metrics.regional_variation: PCFG vs Arctic-migrating populations may differ in vocalization patterns.
song_metrics.key_property: POWERFUL EXPLOSIVE BLOW. FIRST ESCHRICHTIIDAE. Monotypic family. Longest mammal migration. Benthic suction feeder. 13 consecutive marine mammals. 7 families. E=5. v1.49.333.

pelage.overall: Mottled gray with white patches (barnacle scars). No dorsal fin -- series of low bumps (dorsal ridge) along posterior back. Narrow, triangular head. Two blowholes. Heavy barnacle and whale lice infestation.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Mottled gray, heavily barnacled. No dorsal fin (dorsal ridge instead). Narrow head. V-shaped blow. Close to shore during migration.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Barnacle load varies. More barnacles in calving lagoons. Calves born dark gray.
pelage.swimming_pattern: Slow, steady cruiser during migration (3-5 mph). Occasional breaching. Spy-hopping common (raising head above water to look around). Close to shore.

physical_description.overall: Length: 13-15 m. Weight: 30,000-40,000 kg. Robust body. Narrow, triangular head. No dorsal fin. 130-180 short, coarse baleen plates per side. 2-5 ventral grooves (fewer than rorquals).
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Mottled gray, heavily barnacled, no dorsal fin, V-shaped blow, close to shore.
physical_description.field_id: Large mottled gray whale with no dorsal fin, close to shore. V-shaped blow visible from land. Slow, steady swimming. Often barnacle-encrusted. Spy-hopping common.

nodes.title: First Eschrichtiidae -- Monotypic Family
nodes.description: One genus, one species. The Gray Whale is alone in its family. Seventh marine mammal family. Third baleen whale family. Ancient lineage.

nodes.title: Longest Mammal Migration
nodes.description: 10,000-12,000 miles/year. Alaska to Mexico and back. Follows PNW coast closely. Observable from shore. PCFG summer residents.

nodes.title: Benthic Suction Feeding
nodes.description: Rolls on side. Suctions sediment through baleen. Amphipods and worms. Feeding pits. Right-side preference. Unique among baleen whales.

nodes.title: 13 Consecutive Marine Mammals -- 7 Families
nodes.description: Degrees 180-192. 7 families. E=1 to E=8. 5 feeding strategies. Most diverse streak in engine history.

cross_references.relationship: BALEEN PAIR! Humpback (E=8, complex song, lunge) to Gray Whale (E=5, blow, benthic). Different families, different strategies.
THREE BALEEN FAMILIES! Balaenopteridae (190-191) to Eschrichtiidae (192). Three baleen families in three degrees.
THE ENDURING! YFF since 1982. Gray Whale = longest mammal migration (millions of years). Both persist. Total E=10.
